mission.title: traefik: Support for private plugins
mission.description: Support for private plugins. Requested by 51+ users.

steps.title: Check current traefik deployment
steps.description:
Verify your traefik version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik
helm list -n traefik 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working traefik installation.

steps.title: Review traefik configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant traefik configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik
kubectl get configmap -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=traefik
```
### Do you want to request a *feature* or report a *bug*?

Feature

### What did you expect to see?

Support for private plugins. Connecting plugins from private repositories.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support for private plugins
steps.description:
Hey all ,

Im sorry for the long time without another response from our side on that quite important topic here.
 
Be assured, it's certainly not about "letting things rot" but rather, try to come up with a solution :)
First of all, our goal has always been to commit to the Open Source philosophy
```yaml
- Path(/foo) && MyCustomRule(true) -> backend1.example.com
- Path(/foo) && MyCustomRule(false) -> backend1.example.com
```

steps.title: Upgrade traefik to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade traefik:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade traefik traefik/traefik --namespace traefik
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n traefik
helm list -n traefik
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik
kubectl get events -n traefik --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Support for private plugins" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Hey all ,

Im sorry for the long time without another response from our side on that quite important topic here.
 
Be assured, it's certainly not about "letting things rot" but rather, try to come up with a solution :)
First of all, our goal has always been to commit to the Open Source philosophy and thus, Traefik plugins should be open sourced as well.
codeSnippets:
- Path(/foo) && MyCustomRule(true) -> backend1.example.com
- Path(/foo) && MyCustomRule(false) -> backend1.example.com

/path/to/plugins
               |- plugin A
               |- plugin B
               |- Plugin N

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with traefik installed or the issue environment reproducible.
